channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Russian President Vladimir Putin announces an Easter ceasefire in Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin announced a temporary Easter ceasefire in Ukraine, citing humanitarian reasons, the Kremlin said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Members of burned LA church bear a cross through their devastated neighborhood to mark Good Friday
item.description: PACIFIC PALISADES, Calif. (AP) — Carrying a tall wooden cross on his shoulder, Pastor John Shaver walked down the street where his church had stood, just three months ago.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Questions emerge about how a deputy's stepson became the accused gunman in deadly FSU shooting
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Amid the abandoned chemistry notes and other debris left behind after a deadly shooting at Florida State University are lingering questions about how the stepson of a beloved sheriff’s deputy tasked with school safety at a middle school became the accused gunman.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Takeaways from AP’s report on pardoned Jan. 6 rioters being embraced in Republican politics
item.description: JACKSON, Mich. (AP) — Those who rioted, assaulted police officers or broke into congressional offices during the riot at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 are now being received as honored guest speakers at local Republican events around the country.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: America's housing shortage by the numbers
item.description: The U.S. has been short of the homes it needs to meet its growing population for more than a decade, driving up prices and shooting down the dreams of millions of would-be buyers.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Leaders in AOC's 'Red Light' district call on Kash Patel to crush 'worsening' gang crime and prostitution
item.description: Leaders in AOC's "Red Light" District call on FBI chief Kash Patel to crush out-of-control sex trade and drug scourge in Democrat stronghold.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spring allergies are back. Here's how to check pollen levels and keep from sneezing
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Allergy season can be miserable for tens of millions of Americans when trees, grass, and other pollens cause runny noses, itchy eyes, coughing and sneezing.
item.dc:creator: KENYA HUNTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez brothers could get freedom under California law signed by Gavin Newsom: expert
item.description: A California trial lawyer breaks down an analysis of what the judge in the Menendez brothers resentencing hearings is considering when factoring in a potential prison release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge declines to further enforce order restoring AP access to White House
item.description: A federal judge on Friday declined to further enforce his order directing the White House to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces, signaling that while top officials are owed a presumption of good faith, violations of his directive would be met with a heavy hand. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian drummer arrested on child sexual abuse material charges in California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Joseph Seiders, drummer for Canadian indie rock band The New Pornographers, has been arrested and charged with possessing child sex abuse materials, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 67% of CEOs surveyed disapprove of Trump's tariffs
item.description: A survey of more than 300 American business owners and CEOs revealed that 76% of the group believe tariffs will hurt their companies.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montana reports first measles cases since 1990
item.description: Two children have died amid the outbreak this year and a third death is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) and other conservative allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC).
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawsuit challenges Trump administration crackdown on international students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A class action lawsuit filed Friday asks a federal court to reinstate the legal status of international students caught up in a Trump administration crackdown that has left more than a thousand fearful of deportation.
item.dc:creator: ALIA WONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deliberations to resume Monday at bribery trial of Nadine Menendez, wife of ex-US Sen. Bob Menendez
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A jury will resume deliberations Monday in the bribery case brought against Nadine Menendez after a prosecutor told a jury that she was a “critical participant” in a five-year fraud with her husband, former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indiana takes a first -- and long -- shot at acquiring parts of Illinois
item.description: Indiana is taking a first step toward acquiring parts of Illinois, though a last step appears rather unlikely.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ryan Gosling to star in next 'Star Wars' film, Lucasfilm announces
item.description: The Force was with many Japanese, as well as visitors from abroad, at a “Star Wars” event on Friday where Lucasfilm announced the next installation in the film franchise.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, ABC 4

item.title: A US citizen was held for pickup by ICE even after proving he was born in the country
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A U.S. citizen was arrested in Florida for allegedly being in the country illegally and held for pickup by immigration authorities even after his mother showed a judge her son’s birth certificate and the judge dismissed charges.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crews restore power to nearly all clients after island-wide blackout hit Puerto Rico
item.description: Power was restored to 98.8% of clients early Friday after an island-wide blackout hit Puerto earlier this week, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's claims about Abrego Garcia’s gang ties largely rely on 1 confidential tip
item.description: The Trump administration’s argument that the mistakenly deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia is a member of MS-13 is based largely on a confidential tip he and his family have long disputed.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctor in Germany accused of 15 murders, suspected in 95 more
item.description: A 40-year-old doctor practicing palliative medicine in Germany has been charged with killing 15 of his patients and is suspected in another 95 cases, NBC News reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: The NFL Draft is one week away; 49ers, Raiders eye top-valued picks
item.description: The 2025 NFL Draft begins on April 24 and the San Francisco 49ers and the Las Vegas Raiders both have a good shot at snagging top-10 valued talent.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: FSU lecturer finds broken doors in building after 2-hour shooting lockdown
item.description: Busted doors and debris were what a lecturer at Florida State University found after being locked down for hours during Thursday's mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: Check your Venmo, email and mailbox: A payment from a years-old class action lawsuit against Facebook may be on its way this week.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Trump calls FSU shooting a ‘shame,’ says he’ll always protect Second Amendment
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called a deadly shooting at Florida State University (FSU) “terrible,” but he dismissed the possibility of it spurring any changes to gun laws in the country.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: White House proposes eliminating Head Start funding as part of sweeping budget cuts
item.description: The Trump administration is asking Congress to eliminate funding for Head Start, a move that would cut early education for more than half a million of the nation’s neediest children and child care for their families.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Red Robin launches $20 Bottomless Burger Pass for National Burger Month
item.description: May is National Burger Month and Red Robin is celebrating with the launch of its Bottomless Burger Pass — a limited-time offer that gives fans a month of daily gourmet burgers and bottomless sides for just $20. The pass will be available for purchase beginning April 17.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Two dead and 6 injured in Florida State shooting; gunman is deputy's son, police say
item.description: Authorities identified the suspected shooter as a 20-year-old student who is also the son of a local sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WGN 9

item.title: New York family told missing woman was found dead, only to learn she's living in Detroit
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their sister, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Fox 5
